content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 23, 1989

Dick Frank
P.O. Box 996
San Luis Obispo, CA 93406

Re: Your Request for advice
Our File No. A-89-098

Dear Mr. Frank:

This is to confirm our telephone conversation and in reply to
your letter requesting advice concerning your reporting
obligations under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

FACTS

As an elected public official you are required to file a
statement of economic interests. You have the right of
survivorship on certain tax exempt bonds which were purchased and
are controlled by your mother and father. You advised me in our
telephone conversation that all of the bonds are issued by
governmental agencies.

QUESTION

Are you required to report these tax exempt bonds on your
Statement of Economic Interest (Form 730)?

CONCLUSION

Bonds issued by governmental agencies are not reportable.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Mr. Dick Frank
page 2

ANALYSIS

Section 82034 provides that "investment" does not include:
"... any bond or other debt instrument issued by any government or
governmental agency...."

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please
call me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel


by: Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant

February 6, 1989

FPPC
RECD SUM '09

Ms. Coleen McGhee
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Ms. McGhee:

As a locally elected public official, I am required to annually complete Form 730.

Until recently I was unaware that I had been added as a joint tenant with right of survivorship on certain tax exempt bonds purchased and controlled by my mother and father. I was added as the joint tenant for inheritance purposes only and I have no control of the accounts until my parents' demise.

All income from these accounts is credited to their names.

In addition, none of the tax exempt bonds are within the County of my authority and I would have no way of influencing decisions made by the bonding agencies.

As I interpret the instructions for Form 730, these bonds are not required to be reported as (1) they are beyond my jurisdiction, (2) I have no means of influencing .decisions made by the bonding agencies, (3) I do not receive any present beneficial interest, and (4) my joint tenancy is for inheritance purposes only.

Will you please provide me with your analysis and opinion of whether or not these items must be reported? If you need any further clarification, please call me at (805) 549-5646 between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m.

Sincerely,

Dick Frank
Post Office Box 996
San Luis Obispo, California 93406

DF:dlf

824-098

February 6, 1989
FPPC
FEB 10 8 07 AM '89

Ms. Coleen McGhee
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Ms. McGhee:

As a locally elected public official, I am required to annually complete Form 730.

Until recently I was unaware that I had been added as a joint tenant with right of
survivorship on certain tax exempt bonds purchased and controlled by my mother and
father. I was added as the joint tenant for inheritance purposes only and I have
no control of the accounts until my parents' demise.

All income from these accounts is credited to their names.

In addition, none of the tax exempt bonds are within the County of my authority and
I would have no way of influencing decisions made by the bonding agencies.

As I interpret the instructions for Form 730, these bonds are not required to be
reported as (1) they are beyond my jurisdiction, (2) I have no means of influencing
decisions made by the bonding agencies, (3) I do not receive any present beneficial
interest, and (4) my joint tenancy is for inheritance purposes only.

Will you please provide me with your analysis and opinion of whether or not these
items must be reported? If you need any further clarification, please call me at
(805) 549-5646 between the hours of 8:00 a.m. and 5:00 p.m.

Sincerely,

Dick Frank
Post Office Box 996
San Luis Obispo, California 93406

DF:dlf

February 14, 1989


Dick Frank
P.O. Box 996
San Luis Obispo, CA  93406

                                          Re:  Letter No. 89-098

Dear Mr. Frank:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on February 10, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

     You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                          Very truly yours,

                                          [signature]
                                          Jeanne Pritchard
                                          Chief Technical Assistance
                                          and Analysis Division
